Title: Transcription factors TgbHLH95 and TgbZIP44 cotarget terpene biosynthesis gene TgGPPS in Torreya grandis nuts

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Prenyltransferase; contains two Asp-rich DD(X)2-4D motifs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP and DMAPP to produce geranyl diphosphate (GPP), the monoterpene precursor
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream precursor-forming step for monoterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes monoterpene accumulation and positively correlates with terpene content after harvest
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgGPPS transcript abundance was highly and positively correlated with total terpene levels during postharvest ripening. 2. TgGPPS localized to chloroplasts, consistent with plastidial monoterpene precursor synthesis. 3. Transient overexpression in tobacco leaves increased total monoterpenes about 1.6-fold, including α-pinene, myrcene, α-terpinene, and limonene. 4. Stable overexpression in tomato fruit increased monoterpenes 1.3- to 3.3-fold, especially α-pinene and β-pinene.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/FPKM correlation; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; transient overexpression in tobacco leaves; stable overexpression in tomato fruit; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest ripening after harvest under about 20 °C and 90% RH; analyzed at days 0, 4, 8, and 12
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Transient overexpression in tobacco with pGreenII0029 62-SK; stable overexpression in tomato with pART-CAM; promoter activation assays used native TgGPPS promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to enhance nut aroma and monoterpene content through overexpression or promoter activation; can be stacked with upstream transcriptional activators TgbHLH95 and TgbZIP44
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome (Illumina HiSeq 4000); metabolite profiling by GC-MS; specific public accession not reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional testing was performed heterologously in Nicotiana tabacum leaves and Solanum lycopersicum cv. MicroTom fruit, while the gene source and main biological context were Torreya grandis nuts after harvest.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening, similar to TgGPPS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening, similar to TgGPPS
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpene biosynthesis via transcriptional control of monoterpene precursor synthesis gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Indirect transcriptional activation; synergistic promoter activation through protein complex formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates TgGPPS transcription indirectly; strongly enhances TgGPPS promoter activation when coexpressed with TgbZIP44
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a transcriptional complex with TgbZIP44
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that promotes monoterpene accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong positive regulator identified from TF screen; showed strongest individual transactivation among tested TFs and cooperates with TgbZIP44 for stronger TgGPPS activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgbHLH95 was identified among TFs with expression highly similar to TgGPPS. 2. It showed the strongest individual activation of the TgGPPS promoter in dual-luciferase assays (~3.9-fold). 3. It did not directly bind the TgGPPS promoter in yeast one-hybrid, indicating indirect regulation. 4. Transient overexpression in tobacco leaves increased monoterpene content. 5. It physically interacted with TgbZIP44 and together they activated the TgGPPS promoter up to 4.68-fold.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq/RT-qPCR expression profiling; dual-luciferase assay; subcellular localization; yeast one-hybrid; transient overexpression in tobacco leaves; BiFC; LCI; Co-IP; GST pull-down; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest ripening after harvest under about 20 °C and 90% RH; analyzed at days 0, 4, 8, and 12
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector in pGreenII0029 62-SK; tested against native TgGPPS promoter in pGreenII0800-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for improving nut aroma by activating endogenous terpene precursor synthesis; may be most effective when coengineered with TgbZIP44 or TgGPPS
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome; GC-MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although TgbHLH1 also showed evidence of involvement, this paper functionally centered on TgbHLH95 as the major bHLH regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening, similar to TgGPPS
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Kernel/nut after harvest; significantly upregulated from days 4 to 8 during postharvest ripening, similar to TgGPPS
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpene biosynthesis via transcriptional activation of monoterpene precursor synthesis gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct promoter binding and cooperative transcriptional activation in a TF complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds the TgGPPS promoter and, together with TgbHLH95, strongly activates TgGPPS transcription
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a transcriptional complex with TgbHLH95
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Direct positive regulator of TgGPPS that contributes to monoterpene biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct DNA-binding partner that bridges promoter recognition and works with TgbHLH95 to produce the strongest TgGPPS activation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TgbZIP44 was identified by yeast one-hybrid as a TF that directly binds the TgGPPS promoter. 2. EMSA confirmed specific binding to an ACGT-containing promoter element, and mutation abolished binding. 3. TgbZIP44 localized to the nucleus. 4. TgbZIP44 interacted physically with TgbHLH95 in vivo and in vitro. 5. Coexpression with TgbHLH95 induced the TgGPPS promoter to about 4.68-fold, higher than either factor alone.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; dual-luciferase assay; BiFC; LCI; Co-IP; GST pull-down
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Postharvest ripening after harvest under about 20 °C and 90% RH; analyzed at days 0, 4, 8, and 12
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Effector in pGreenII0029 62-SK; tested against native TgGPPS promoter in pGreenII0800-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising direct regulator for engineering nut aroma by activating TgGPPS; likely most useful in combination with TgbHLH95 to maximize promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TgbZIP44 alone had limited promoter activation in the synergy assay, but serves as the direct DNA-binding component of the activating complex.
